edits.rationale: The definition of 'FAI' and 'LOTO' has been updated in v2 to clarify their context in quality assurance.
edits.new_text: as defined in AIQS 1.3.3 — FAI and LOTO definitions

edits.rationale: AIQS 1.5.2 was not found in v2 and no similar replacement clause was identified. The clause may have been deprecated — human review required.
edits.old_text: Personnel must complete training on supplier qualification processes and be familiar with AIQS standards, particularly AIQS 1.1.2 and AIQS 1.5.2. Additionally, personnel shall have access to the Supplier Qualification Checklist and the Supplier Evaluation Form. Prior to initiating the qualification process, ensure that the necessary tools and equipment, including the Precision Surface Grinder (2847.310.0055) and Vertical Turning Lathe (2847.310.0061), are operational and compliant with safety regulations. Conduct a Lockout-Tagout (LOTO) procedure if maintenance is required on any equipment.

edits.rationale: The clause content has been updated in v2 to include specific requirements for training programs and records of training completion.
edits.old_text: Personnel must complete training on supplier qualification processes and be familiar with AIQS standards, particularly AIQS 1.1.2 and AIQS 1.5.2.
edits.new_text: Personnel shall be applicable to all employees, contractors, and third-party vendors involved in the manufacturing process. Training programs shall be established to ensure that all relevant personnel understand their responsibilities under this standard.

edits.rationale: The definition of AIQS 1.3.3 has changed in v2 and needs to be updated to reflect the new content.
edits.new_text: AIQS 1.3.3 — FAI and LOTO definitions 'FAI' and 'LOTO' shall be defined in the context of quality assurance as follows: 'FAI' refers to First Article Inspection, a process that verifies that the first production unit meets all specified requirements before full-scale manufacturing begins.

edits.rationale: AIQS 1.5.2 was not found in v2 and no similar replacement clause was identified. The clause may have been deprecated — human review required.
edits.old_text: Maintain all records related to the supplier qualification process, including the Supplier Qualification Checklist, Supplier Evaluation Form, FAI reports, and any NCRs. These records shall be stored in the designated quality management system database for a minimum of five years, as per AIQS 1.5.2. Ensure that records are easily accessible for audits and reviews, and that they are updated as necessary to reflect any changes in supplier status or performance.
